claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch system implements full provider-adapter boundary control for Claude/Codex/custom agents with post-execution snapshot auditing, comprehensive test coverage showing dual-gate execution authorization and provider isolation, and active runtime evidence generation in .yolo/demand/evidence/.

evidence.summary: CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS defines complete tool allowlist including Write, Edit, Bash, Agent, TaskCreate, TaskUpdate. DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS sets permissions.allow to this array.
evidence.why: Core boundary control mechanism - defines what tools evidence agents can access by default.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot walks project files (excluding .git, node_modules, dist) and computes SHA256 digests for change detection.
evidence.why: Foundation for post-execution boundary audit - creates before/after snapshots for mutation detection.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots and returns added, modified, deleted file changes.
evidence.why: Implements the boundary violation detection logic that converts mutations to blockers.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime requires execute=true AND allowAgentDispatch=true for execution, builds boundary snapshot before agents, runs agents sequentially, then builds after snapshot and diffs for mutations.
evidence.why: Core runtime implementing dual-gate execution policy and boundary auditing workflow.

evidence.summary: After agent execution, boundary.mutation is set to 'violated' if changes detected, with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers added to readiness.
evidence.why: Conversion of boundary violations to PRD readiness blockers - the enforcement mechanism.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agentToolProfile to 'boundary' (default), 'research', or 'full' (requires allowFullAgentTools). For Claude, applies DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS, sets claude_tools=default, claude_allowed_tools=CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS, claude_no_session_persistence=true.
evidence.why: Provider-specific configuration logic showing how boundary settings are applied per provider type.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation for Claude constructs CLI: -p, --model, --permission-mode, --settings (JSON path), --tools, --allowedTools, --disallowedTools, --disable-slash-commands, --no-session-persistence, --max-budget-usd.
evidence.why: Translation layer converting boundary config into actual Claude CLI arguments.

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt spawns Claude process with invocation args, pipes prompt to stdin, collects stdout/stderr, handles timeout, returns structured result.
evidence.why: Process execution layer showing how evidence agents are spawned and their output captured.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross_checker, verifier with objective, prompt, writes_code=false, result_schema, required_when conditions.
evidence.why: Formal specification of evidence agent protocols showing read-only intent.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceTasks creates agent task list based on evidence_policy: none=[], single_agent=[explorer, verifier], cross_check=[explorer, cross-checker, verifier].
evidence.why: Routing logic showing how evidence policy determines which agents must run.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues converts missing[], recommendation in ['clarify','blocked','block'], verdict in ['blocked','block','fail'], status in ['blocked','failed','error'] into blockers.
evidence.why: Evidence-to-blocker conversion showing how agent recommendations affect PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement groups results by claim, detects conflicts (block+proceed, or proceed+clarify), returns blocked status with EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT blockers.
evidence.why: Conflict detection mechanism preventing averaging-away of disagreements between evidence agents.

evidence.summary: Tests verify buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan creates ready status with explorer/cross-checker/verifier actions, dry-run doesn't spawn agents.
evidence.why: Test evidence for planning and dry-run behavior.

evidence.summary: Tests confirm execute mode requires both executeAgents AND allowAgentDispatch (dual-gate), CLI defaults to dry-run.
evidence.why: Test evidence for dual-gate execution authorization.

evidence.summary: Test runs full explorer→cross-checker→verifier workflow with fake agent outputs, verifies readiness.prd_ready=true on success.
evidence.why: Test evidence for complete evidence workflow integration.

evidence.summary: Test verifies codex dispatch preserves provider selection, doesn't inherit claude default model, allows unset sandbox defaults.
evidence.why: Test evidence for provider-specific isolation.

evidence.summary: Test verifies claude dispatch keeps tools available (default), relies on boundary enforcement, no-session-persistence, max-budget-usd passed through.
evidence.why: Test evidence for Claude provider configuration and tool availability.

evidence.summary: Tests verify full tool profile requires explicit allowFullAgentTools authorization, otherwise defaults to boundary profile.
evidence.why: Test evidence for tool profile gating and default safety.

evidence.summary: Test verifies research profile labels correctly, allows WebFetch/WebSearch alongside boundary tools.
evidence.why: Test evidence for research tool profile handling.

evidence.summary: Test simulates agent writing project file outside allowed root, verifies boundary.violated, readiness blocked with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION.
evidence.why: Critical test evidence for boundary enforcement preventing project mutation.

evidence.summary: Test simulates explorer vs cross-checker conflict (proceed vs block), verifies EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT blocker keeps readiness blocked.
evidence.why: Test evidence for conflict handling and blocker persistence.

evidence.summary: 10+ dispatch directories (dispatch-YYYYMMDDHHMMSS-*) each containing explorer.json, cross-checker.json, verifier.json, dispatch.json artifacts.
evidence.why: Runtime evidence showing system is actively generating evidence artifacts.

evidence.summary: Package defines YOLO as automation pipeline with dependencies, exports demand router/evidence-dispatch, includes demand-* test scripts.
evidence.why: Project context showing YOLO structure and demand system integration.

evidence.summary: README describes YOLO as lifecycle agent with gate/evidence boundaries, core flow from idea through discovery to ship/learn.
evidence.why: Product documentation confirming boundary-first design philosophy.

assumptions: CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS array remains complete and current for Claude Code's actual tool set
Post-execution boundary snapshot detects all relevant mutation types (files, symlinks, permissions, metadata)
Provider isolation in executionConfig prevents cross-provider model/state leakage
Evidence agent outputs follow required JSON schema in production (not just in tests)
Real Claude -p agents respect claude_settings permissions.allow configuration as tested
Boundary snapshot excludes .git, node_modules, dist sufficiently to ignore non-project changes
risks: If CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS is incomplete or outdated, agents may access unexpected tools
If boundary snapshot has false negatives (misses mutations), project files can be corrupted without triggering blockers
If provider isolation fails, cross-provider state leakage could cause unexpected behavior
If real Claude agents ignore boundary settings despite tests passing, project mutation could occur
If evidence agents return malformed JSON or don't follow schema, normalization may lose important data
missing: Runtime verification with real Claude -p agents that boundary settings actually restrict tool usage in production
Integration tests showing provider isolation prevents cross-provider model leakage
Evidence that CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS matches Claude Code's current available tool set
Verification that boundary snapshot detects all mutation types (symlink, permissions, metadata, etc.)
Production evidence logs showing boundary violations caught and blocked
Cross-check of recent dispatch artifacts showing agent adherence to read-only protocol

result.notes: Source code, tests, and runtime artifacts provide comprehensive evidence that demand evidence dispatch implements provider-adapter boundary control, post-execution snapshot auditing, dual-gate execution authorization, and conflict handling. All tests pass. However, key assumptions remain about production behavior: real Claude agents respecting boundary settings, snapshot detecting all mutation types, and CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS completeness. Acceptance criteria for this demand specifically requires cross-check verification before PRD readiness.
